title: The Banshees of Inisherin
logline: On a remote Irish island in 1923, a simple farmer's world crumbles when his lifelong best friend suddenly and inexplicably ends their friendship, leading to an escalating feud that destroys everything they hold dear.
antagonistOrConflict: Colm Doherty and the existential crisis of meaninglessness
themes: The meaning of life and legacy
The cost of artistic pursuit vs. simple human connection

actBreaks.description: Colm makes his ultimatum threat to cut off his fingers if Padraic continues to bother him, escalating the conflict from rejection to potential violence.

validation.ruling: Scene #50 shows Colm's shocking ultimatum to cut off his own fingers if Padraic continues bothering him, fundamentally escalating the stakes and changing the story's direction from a simple friendship dispute to a life-threatening conflict.

actBreaks.description: After Jenny's death and the final confrontation in the pub, Padraic threatens to burn down Colm's house, marking the point of no return.

validation.ruling: Scene #132 shows a sunset montage across the island's locations, serving as a transitional moment before the final act where Padraic will take his ultimate revenge, marking the end of Act 2 with atmospheric finality.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a tightly wound chamber piece that uses the claustrophobic island setting to intensify a simple premise into tragic proportions. McDonagh structures it as a slow-burn escalation where each act raises the stakes exponentially - from social rejection to physical mutilation to total destruction. The story moves with the inexorable logic of Greek tragedy, where both men's stubbornness drives them toward mutual annihilation. The pacing is deliberately measured, allowing small moments of island life to accumulate weight and meaning before each explosive turn.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains steady momentum through short scenes (average 1.1 pages) that create a sense of relentless forward motion. Key pacing shifts occur around scene 76 (the first finger) and scene 117 (Siobhan's departure), where the tempo accelerates toward the final catastrophe. The montage sequences (scenes 90-96) provide breathing room while showing the passage of time and deepening isolation.

incitingIncident.description: Padraic confronts Colm outside the pub, demanding to know what he's done wrong and promising to apologize, but Colm tells him he didn't do or say anything wrong.

validation.ruling: Scene #4 shows only Padraic asking Colm to come to the pub, but doesn't contain Colm's rejection that shatters their friendship. Scene #15 is the true inciting incident where Colm explicitly tells Padraic he doesn't like him anymore and doesn't want to be friends, completely destroying Padraic's ordinary world and establishing the main conflict.

midpoint.description: Blood seeps through a shoebox containing the finger as Siobhan demands Padraic leave Colm alone for good, but Padraic insists on returning it.

validation.ruling: Scene #89 shows Padraic and Siobhan eating breakfast with Colm's severed finger in a bloody shoebox on the table, marking Padraic's shift from reactive to proactive as he refuses to heed Siobhan's warnings to leave Colm alone and instead plans to return the finger, escalating the conflict to a point of no return.

climax.description: Peadar strides away from Colm's burning house taking out his handcuffs, heading toward Padraic's house.
climax.sceneHeading: 152 EXT. HILL ABOVE COLM’S HOUSE - DUSK 152 152

validation.ruling: Scene #147 is merely a transitional moment after church, not a confrontation. Scene #163 is the true climax where Padraic directly confronts Colm at his burning house, delivering his ultimatum that they are now even and will be enemies for life, representing the peak dramatic tension and final resolution of their conflict.

scenes.heading: 1 EXT. VARIOUS ISLAND LOCATIONS - DAY 1
scenes.summary: Padraic Suilleabhain walks through various locations on the island of Inisherin in 1923, passing cottages, ruins, and a lake.

scenes.heading: 2 EXT. HILL ABOVE COLM’S HOUSE - DAY 2
scenes.summary: Padraic approaches a lonely cottage overlooking a beach with smoke rising from its chimney.

scenes.heading: 3 EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - DAY 3
scenes.summary: Padraic arrives at Colm's cottage, pats the dog, knocks on the door with no response, and looks through the window.

scenes.heading: 4 INT. COLM’S HOUSE - DAY 4
scenes.summary: Padraic sees Colm sitting in an armchair smoking and asks if he's coming to the pub.

scenes.heading: 5 EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 5
scenes.summary: Padraic finds the door locked, speaks to Colm through the window about going to the pub, but Colm ignores him completely and continues smoking.
scenes.turningPointNote: First clear sign that something has fundamentally changed in their friendship

scenes.heading: 6 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 6
scenes.summary: Padraic returns home confused and tells his sister Siobhan that Colm was just sitting there smoking and doing nothing.

scenes.heading: 7 EXT. LANEWAYS - DAY 7
scenes.summary: Padraic greets Officer Peadar Kearney while walking, but the policeman completely ignores him.

scenes.heading: 8 EXT. PUB - DAY 8
scenes.summary: Padraic arrives at the local pub, a lonely building overlooking the sea.

scenes.heading: 9 INT. PUB - DAY 9
scenes.summary: Padraic orders a pint from Jonjo, who questions why Colm isn't with him and seems confused when Padraic explains Colm was just sitting there smoking.

scenes.heading: 10 EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - DAY 10
scenes.summary: Padraic returns to Colm's house, finds no one in the armchair, knocks again, and discovers the door is now open.

scenes.heading: 11 INT. COLM’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 11
scenes.summary: Padraic enters Colm's empty house, uses Colm's telescope to look out the window, and sees Colm walking away up a distant hill.

scenes.heading: 12 EXT. LANEWAYS - DAY 12
scenes.summary: Padraic chases after Colm through winding lanes but loses sight of him, then sees Colm scaling walls and threatening a bull in a field.

scenes.heading: 13 EXT. LANEWAY TO PUB - DAY 13
scenes.summary: Padraic trudges back to the pub and sees through the window that Colm is inside laughing with Jonjo and Gerry.

scenes.heading: 14 INT. PUB - DAY 14
scenes.summary: Padraic enters the pub smiling, but Colm immediately loses his jocularity and tells him to sit somewhere else, then leaves to sit outside.

scenes.heading: 15 EXT. PUB - DAY 15
scenes.summary: Padraic confronts Colm outside the pub, demanding to know what he's done wrong and promising to apologize, but Colm tells him he didn't do or say anything wrong.
scenes.turningPointNote: Colm reveals the rejection isn't about anything Padraic did, deepening the mystery and Padraic's confusion

scenes.heading: 16 EXT. LANEWAYS - DAY 16
scenes.summary: Padraic walks lost in thought and encounters Dominic Kearney, who shows him a stick with a hook and walks alongside him chatting.

scenes.heading: 17 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 17
scenes.summary: Padraic sits staring into space at home while Siobhan enters with groceries, surprised to find him there when the pub was open.

scenes.heading: 18 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DUSK 18
scenes.summary: A moonlit night scene showing the house with washing in the breeze and animals sleeping.

scenes.heading: 19 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DUSK 19
scenes.summary: Padraic, Siobhan, and elderly neighbor Mrs McCormick sit together as Siobhan sews and Mrs McCormick asks about their parents' death seven years ago.

scenes.heading: 20 EXT. HIGH LANEWAYS - NIGHT (DUSK?) 20
scenes.summary: Padraic observes cannon-fire and smoke rising from the mainland civil war in the distance.

scenes.heading: 21 EXT. PUB - NIGHT 21
scenes.summary: Padraic arrives at the pub surprised to hear music and liveliness inside.

scenes.heading: 22 INT. PUB - NIGHT 22
scenes.summary: Colm plays fiddle in a crowded session with local women around him, while Padraic and Dominic watch from a window table, both getting drunk.

scenes.heading: 23 INT. DOMINIC’S HOUSE - NIGHT 23
scenes.summary: Dominic quietly steals poteen from his naked, sleeping policeman father while Padraic tries not to look at the disturbing scene.

scenes.heading: 24 EXT. CASTLE RUINS (AT DUN AENGUS) - NIGHT 24
scenes.summary: Padraic and Dominic drink the stolen poteen at castle ruins overlooking cliffs, discussing the civil war and Dominic asking about Siobhan.

scenes.heading: 25 INT. PADRAIC/SIOBHAN’S BEDROOM - DAWN 25
scenes.summary: Siobhan sleeps while Padraic lies awake unable to sleep as sunrise breaks through their bedroom window.

scenes.heading: 26 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAWN 26
scenes.summary: Padraic lets his donkey inside, watches the sunrise, and realizes while looking at the calendar that yesterday was April Fool's Day.

scenes.heading: 27 EXT. HIGH LANEWAYS - DAWN 27
scenes.summary: A happy Padraic walks his cows along the hill and spots Colm in the distance leaning on a wall with his fiddle.

scenes.heading: 28 EXT. HIGH LANEWAYS - DAWN 28
scenes.summary: Padraic approaches Colm with his cows, trying to make casual conversation about the date change and April Fool's Day, but Colm responds blankly.

scenes.heading: 29 EXT. PRETTY PASTURE OVERLOOKING SEA - DAY 29
scenes.summary: Padraic happily puts his cows in a new field, talking optimistically about the new day being April 2nd.

scenes.heading: 30 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 30
scenes.summary: Padraic shaves and dresses cheerfully while Siobhan reads, and she asks him if he ever gets lonely.

scenes.heading: 31 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 31
scenes.summary: Padraic walks away from his house past Jenny the donkey, muttering about Siobhan's question about loneliness.

scenes.heading: 32 INT. COLM’S HOUSE - DAY 32
scenes.summary: Colm tries unsuccessfully to compose music on his fiddle, then makes a screeching din that frightens his dog before stopping and staring in depression.

scenes.heading: 33 EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - DAY 33
scenes.summary: Padraic knocks on Colm's door but sees him striding away across the hill with his fiddle and dog, realizing the situation is serious.

scenes.heading: 34 EXT. LANEWAY TO PUB - DAY 34
scenes.summary: Padraic walks to the pub with all happiness gone.

scenes.heading: 35 INT. PUB - DAY 35
scenes.summary: Padraic enters the pub where Colm ignores his nod, then sits at Colm's table uninvited, leading to Colm asking what he's doing.

scenes.heading: 36 EXT. PUB - DAY 36
scenes.summary: Padraic sits alone outside feeling like crying when Colm joins him and explains he needs to spend his remaining time thinking and composing instead of listening to dull conversation.
scenes.turningPointNote: Colm directly explains his rejection of Padraic, calling him dull and revealing his existential crisis about time running out

scenes.heading: 37 EXT. PRETTY LANE NEAR THE PUB (OR ANYWHERE) - DUSK 37
scenes.summary: Siobhan, dressed up for their planned sherry, meets a mopey Padraic who says he doesn't feel like going out.

scenes.heading: 38 INT/EXT. PUB - DUSK 38
scenes.summary: Siobhan bursts into the pub and angrily confronts Colm about his treatment of her brother, demanding to know what's going on.

scenes.heading: 39 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DUSK 39
scenes.summary: Padraic feeds his animals while Siobhan returns from the pub lost in thought, unable to give him a proper smile.

scenes.heading: 40 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - NIGHT 40
scenes.summary: During dinner, Padraic asks Siobhan if she thinks he's dull, and she reassures him he's nice, while he worries about people laughing at him behind his back.

scenes.heading: 41 INT. PADRAIC’S BEDROOM - NIGHT 41
scenes.summary: Padraic and Siobhan lie awake in their twin beds unable to sleep, with distant sounds of cannon-fire or thunder.

scenes.heading: 42 EXT. CHURCH - DAWN 42
scenes.summary: Church bells ring across the island calling everyone to Mass.

scenes.heading: 43 EXT. LANEWAYS - DAWN 43
scenes.summary: All the islanders walk to church in response to the bells.

scenes.heading: 44 EXT. JETTY - DAWN 44
scenes.summary: The priest arrives by boat and is warmly greeted by Peadar, who escorts him to church with his arm around the priest's shoulder.

scenes.heading: 45 EXT. HIGH LANEWAYS - DAWN 45
scenes.summary: Padraic and Siobhan encounter a bruised and bloody Dominic who explains his father beat him with a kettle over the stolen poteen.

scenes.heading: 46 INT. CHURCH - DAY 46
scenes.summary: During Mass, Padraic notices people glancing at him and overhears snippets of conversation about Colm stopping speaking to him overnight.

scenes.heading: 47 EXT. CHURCH - DAY 47
scenes.summary: After church, Padraic whispers something to the confused priest while shaking his hand.

scenes.heading: 48 INT. CONFESSIONAL - DAY 48
scenes.summary: Colm confesses his usual sins to the priest, who asks about his despair and suggests he stop being friends with Padraic if it helps.

scenes.heading: 49 EXT. LANEWAY TO PUB - DAY 49
scenes.summary: Colm angrily strides to the pub where Padraic's cart is tied up with other Sunday churchgoers, ignoring their greetings.

scenes.heading: 50 INT. PUB - DAY 50
scenes.summary: Colm enters the crowded pub and threatens Padraic that he will cut off his own fiddle fingers with shears and give them to him each time Padraic bothers him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Colm escalates to a shocking, self-destructive ultimatum that threatens his musical ability and creates an irreversible stakes

scenes.heading: 51 EXT. HIGH LANEWAYS - DAY 51
scenes.summary: Padraic rides his pony cart through drizzly rain and encounters Mrs McCormick leaning against a wall, smiling knowingly at him.

scenes.heading: 52 EXT. VARIOUS - DUSK 52
scenes.summary: Storm clouds and rain sweep over various parts of the island including castle ruins, lake, laneways, and near Padraic's home.

scenes.heading: 53 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - NIGHT 53
scenes.summary: Rain, thunder and lightning strike outside Padraic's house as he, Siobhan and Dominic sit around the dinner table inside.

scenes.heading: 54 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - NIGHT 54
scenes.summary: During dinner, bruised Dominic eats loudly and mocks Colm while depressed Padraic can barely eat and Siobhan worries about him.

scenes.heading: 55 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAWN 55
scenes.summary: Padraic loads milk churns onto his cart and rides off at dawn.

scenes.heading: 56 EXT. LANEWAY OUTSIDE TOWN - DAWN 56
scenes.summary: Padraic rides along with milk churns as the sun rises, revealing the island's small thatched town for the first time.

scenes.heading: 57 EXT. SHOP/POST OFFICE - DAY 57
scenes.summary: Padraic ties his pony and cart outside the shop, rolls a milk churn to the door and enters.

scenes.heading: 58 INT. SHOP/POST OFFICE - DAY 58
scenes.summary: Padraic delivers milk to Mrs O'Riordan's shop where she complains about lack of news from his side of the island while Mrs McCormick sits oddly nearby.

scenes.heading: 59 EXT. SHOP/POST OFFICE - DAY 59
scenes.summary: Padraic quickly unties his cart, sees Colm walking distantly, then Peadar arrives and punches him twice in the head, demanding his son return home.

scenes.heading: 60 EXT. HIGH LANEWAY TO CROSSROADS - DAY 60
scenes.summary: Colm helps the bloodied Padraic by taking the reins as they ride together, with Padraic breaking into uncontrollable sobs until Colm stops at a crossroads and gently takes his hand.

scenes.heading: 61 EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - DAY 61
scenes.summary: Colm sits outside his house playing a beautiful second part to his new composition on the fiddle, ending abruptly but feeling satisfied.

scenes.heading: 62 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 62
scenes.summary: Padraic watches Dominic leave sadly with half a loaf, then lets his little donkey into the house.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 63
scenes.summary: Padraic sits bruised and depressed on the floor until his donkey cheers him up, inspiring him to decide they'll go to the pub together.

scenes.heading: 64 EXT. HIGH LANEWAYS (OR ANYWHERE) - DUSK 64
scenes.summary: Padraic walks the lanes with his donkey beside him as the sun sets behind them.

scenes.heading: 65 INT. PUB - DUSK 65
scenes.summary: At the pub, Peadar throws a coin at Dominic's head and tells him he can have one drink before going home to iron a shirt.

scenes.heading: 66 EXT. PUB - DUSK/NIGHT 66
scenes.summary: Padraic arrives outside the pub and sees Colm's dog, watching sadly as his donkey and the dog affectionately greet each other.

scenes.heading: INT. PUB - NIGHT 67A
scenes.summary: Drunk Padraic confronts Colm in the crowded pub, calling him no longer nice while Colm argues that music and art last longer than niceness.

scenes.heading: 68 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 68
scenes.summary: Dominic rushes to Padraic's house and tells Siobhan that Padraic is drunk and confronting Colm at the pub.

scenes.heading: 69 INT. PUB - CONTINUOUS 69
scenes.summary: Padraic continues his drunken confrontation with Colm about niceness versus lasting art as Siobhan and Dominic arrive at the pub.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND (INISHMORE) - DAWN 69A
scenes.summary: Rain lashes the island at dawn.

scenes.heading: 70 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAWN 70
scenes.summary: Padraic's house sits in the rain at dawn as animals sleep in the barn and a cock crows, waking Padraic inside.

scenes.heading: 71 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAWN 71
scenes.summary: Hungover Padraic wraps a wet towel around his head and notices the calendar marked with his parents' death date, April 5th.

scenes.heading: 72 INT. SHOP/POST OFFICE - DAWN 72
scenes.summary: Siobhan enters the shop for rashers and receives a letter that Mrs O'Riordan has already steamed open and read.

scenes.heading: 73 EXT. GRAVEYARD - MORNING 73
scenes.summary: Hungover Padraic visits his parents' grave, lays flowers, vomits on the grass, then sees Peadar on a departing boat in military uniform.

scenes.heading: 74 EXT. BOAT - MORNING 74
scenes.summary: Peadar makes a slow throat-slitting gesture with his finger toward Padraic from the departing boat.

scenes.heading: 75 EXT. GRAVEYARD - MORNING 75
scenes.summary: Padraic remembers the previous night's events and sees Colm on the beach below with his dog, then decides whether to approach him.

scenes.heading: 76 EXT. BEACH BY GRAVEYARD - MORNING 76
scenes.summary: Padraic approaches Colm on the beach to apologize for his drunken behavior, but Colm pretends not to remember what happened.

scenes.heading: 77 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 77
scenes.summary: Hungover Padraic sits depressed while his donkey eats a carrot from his hand as Siobhan returns with groceries and a letter.

scenes.heading: 78 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 78
scenes.summary: Padraic hears a knock and sees no one outside, then spots Colm in the distance climbing walls with something white wrapped around his hand.

scenes.heading: 79 EXT. FIELD - CONTINUOUS 79
scenes.summary: Padraic notices blood spatter on his door and discovers Colm's severed index finger lying in the grass below.
scenes.turningPointNote: Colm has cut off his own finger and left it at Padraic's door, escalating their conflict to shocking self-mutilation

scenes.heading: 80 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 80
scenes.summary: Colm continues walking across fields with his bandaged hand, seemingly unperturbed by cutting off his finger.

scenes.heading: 81 EXT. FIELD - CONTINUOUS 81
scenes.summary: Colm scales another wall and continues along the lane after leaving his severed finger at Padraic's door.

scenes.heading: 82 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 82
scenes.summary: Padraic shows Siobhan the bloody severed finger, causing her to scream in horror and demand he throw it out.

scenes.heading: 83 INT. COLM’S HOUSE - DAY 83
scenes.summary: Colm sits smoking in his house with bloody shears nearby as his dog licks the wound where his index finger used to be, then plays his fiddle.

scenes.heading: 84 EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 84
scenes.summary: The sound of Colm's fiddle tune drifts across the island as rain begins to fall on his house.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASTLE RUINS - CONTINUOUS 85A
scenes.summary: Rain continues to drench the castle ruins while Colm's music plays.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKE - DAY 85B
scenes.summary: Mrs McCormick and an old packhorse stand staring blankly at the lake as Colm's tune continues playing.

scenes.heading: EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 85C
scenes.summary: Colm ends his tune abruptly, hangs up his fiddle and exits, leaving the instrument and bloody shears framed in his rain-lashed window.

scenes.heading: 86 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 86
scenes.summary: Blood seeps through a shoebox containing the finger as Siobhan demands Padraic leave Colm alone for good, but Padraic insists on returning it.

scenes.heading: 87 EXT. BEACH - DUSK 87
scenes.summary: Siobhan walks along the beach toward Colm's house carrying the shoebox when distant rifle fire from the mainland stops her momentarily.

scenes.heading: 88 OMITTED 88
scenes.summary: This scene is marked as omitted.

scenes.heading: 89 INT. COLM’S HOUSE - DUSK 89
scenes.summary: Siobhan visits Colm and returns his severed finger in the shoebox, asking what he needs from Padraic to end their conflict.

scenes.heading: 90 EXT. VARIOUS - DUSK 90
scenes.summary: A montage shows lonely island places at sunset including the graveyard, lake, both houses and sleeping animals.

scenes.heading: 91 EXT. PRETTY PASTURE OVERLOOKING SEA - DAWN 91
scenes.summary: Sad Padraic collects his cows at sunrise and passes Colm on the lane, who looks away and ignores him completely.

scenes.heading: 92 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 92
scenes.summary: Padraic and Siobhan sit in their house as she writes a letter and he stares into space, with him staying put when the clock strikes two.

scenes.heading: 93 EXT. SHOP/POST OFFICE - DAY 93
scenes.summary: Siobhan seals and posts her letter at the shop while Mrs O'Riordan and Mrs McCormick watch with irritation.

scenes.heading: 94 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 94
scenes.summary: Padraic sadly feeds his animals who sense something is wrong, then sighs and looks across the island.

scenes.heading: 95 INT. PUB - DUSK 95
scenes.summary: At the pub, Padraic drinks alone while watching Colm read and chat with student musician Declan, then quietly leaves.

scenes.heading: 96 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 96
scenes.summary: Padraic stares into space at home as Siobhan returns and closes herself in her room, unable to help him out of his depression.

scenes.heading: 97 EXT. HIGH LANEWAY TO CROSSROADS - DAY 97
scenes.summary: Padraic gives student musician Declan a ride and lies about receiving a telegram saying Declan's father was hit by a bread van.

scenes.heading: 98 EXT. BOAT/JETTY - DAY 98
scenes.summary: Peadar returns on a boat as mist rolls in and notices something at the jetty.

scenes.heading: 99 EXT. JETTY - CONTINUOUS 99
scenes.summary: Siobhan finishes discussing payment with a boatman while Peadar questions her and threatens to beat up her brother.

scenes.heading: 100 EXT. LANEWAY NEAR GRAVEYARD - NIGHT (DUSK?) 100 100
scenes.summary: Padraic hides from Mrs McCormick in a graveyard but she appears right beside him and prophesies that a death will come to Inisherin before the month is out.

scenes.heading: 101 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - NIGHT 101 101
scenes.summary: Padraic wakes to hear Siobhan crying in bed, asks her to cry quieter, then feels bad about the resulting silence.

scenes.heading: EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - NIGHT 101A
scenes.summary: The camera pans from their bedroom window across a moonlit ocean to sunrise.

scenes.heading: INT. COLM’S HOUSE - NIGHT 101AA
scenes.summary: Colm sits in moonlight looking contentedly at where his finger used to be.

scenes.heading: EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - NIGHT/DAWN 101AB
scenes.summary: The camera pans back to Padraic's window as he awakens at dawn.

scenes.heading: INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 101B
scenes.summary: Padraic discovers Siobhan's bed is empty and made, searches the house but doesn't find her.

scenes.heading: 102 INT/EXT. DOMINIC’S HOUSE - DAWN 102 102
scenes.summary: Peadar lies naked on bloodied sheets telling Dominic about boring executions while Dominic gets dressed and quietly leaves with a bottle.

scenes.heading: 103 EXT. CASTLE RUINS AT THE BLACK FORT - DAWN 103 103
scenes.summary: Padraic and Dominic sit drinking at dawn while Dominic warns that his father plans to beat up Padraic on Sunday and Padraic apologizes for revealing the abuse.

scenes.heading: 104 EXT. BEACH - DAY 104 104
scenes.summary: Padraic finishes the poteen bottle on the beach, tosses it into the surf, and heads toward Colm's smoking house.

scenes.heading: 105 OMITTED 105 105
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 106 EXT. LAKE - DAY 106 106
scenes.summary: Siobhan stands at a foggy lake and sees Mrs McCormick across the water making a creepy beckoning gesture before Dominic startles her by appearing.

scenes.heading: 107 INT. COLM’S HOUSE - DAY 107 107
scenes.summary: Colm dances and sings an Irish song with his reluctant dog inside his house.

scenes.heading: 108 EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 108 108
scenes.summary: Padraic watches Colm dancing with his dog through the window, his heart leaping at the joyful sight, and walks away from the window.

scenes.heading: 109 INT. COLM’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 109 109
scenes.summary: Padraic kicks open Colm's door and confronts him aggressively, mocking him for dancing with his dog and boasting about having ten fingers while Colm admits to having nine.
scenes.turningPointNote: Padraic's aggressive confrontation escalates the conflict to a breaking point

scenes.heading: INT. COLM’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 109A
scenes.summary: Colm's dog takes the bloody shears in his mouth and drags them toward the door while Colm lovingly pats him and takes the shears upstairs.

scenes.heading: 110 INT. PUB - DAY 110 110
scenes.summary: Padraic orders two pints and sits at Colm's usual table to wait for his friend while Jonjo questions his behavior.

scenes.heading: 111 EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - DAY 111 111
scenes.summary: Colm leaves his house as dense fog rolls in, walking away while his dog barks from inside.

scenes.heading: 112 INT. PUB - DAY 112 112
scenes.summary: Padraic continues waiting at the pub as the clock strikes four until Siobhan arrives and questions what he's doing.

scenes.heading: 113 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 113 113
scenes.summary: Colm approaches Padraic's empty house through the fog and throws something at the door with a thud.

scenes.heading: 114 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 114 114
scenes.summary: Colm throws his remaining fingers and thumb at Padraic's door, each sliding down the bloody surface.
scenes.turningPointNote: Colm's self-mutilation reaches its climax as he throws all his remaining fingers

scenes.heading: 115 EXT. HIGH LANEWAYS - DAY 115 115
scenes.summary: Padraic follows Siobhan through fog, worried about having no friends left and asking who will do the cooking when she leaves.

scenes.heading: 116 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 116 116
scenes.summary: Padraic and Siobhan discover blood spatters on their door but cannot find the fingers in the fog.

scenes.heading: 117 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 117 117
scenes.summary: Siobhan packs her suitcase while Padraic begs her not to leave, and she asks him to look after her books before saying goodbye.
scenes.turningPointNote: Siobhan's departure marks a major loss for Padraic and escalates his isolation

scenes.heading: 118 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 118 118
scenes.summary: Siobhan looks back at Padraic, their house, and the animals from the bend in the road before disappearing.

scenes.heading: 119 EXT. JETTY - DAY 119 119
scenes.summary: Siobhan waits at the jetty with a crying young man named Declan, both boarding the ferry as it pulls away from Inisherin.

scenes.heading: 120 EXT. BOAT - DAY 120 120
scenes.summary: From the boat, Siobhan sees Padraic waving goodbye from the cliff edge near the castle ruins and waves back tearfully.

scenes.heading: 121 EXT. CASTLE RUINS - CONTINUOUS 121 121
scenes.summary: Padraic stands dangerously close to the cliff edge watching Siobhan's distant boat as a shadow flits across his back.

scenes.heading: 122 EXT. BOAT - CONTINUOUS 122 122
scenes.summary: Siobhan watches Padraic from the boat, concerned about his proximity to the cliff edge and noticing Mrs McCormick nearby, until he waves and steps back safely.

scenes.heading: 123 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 123 123
scenes.summary: Padraic follows a trail of blood around his house and discovers his dead donkey with Colm's thumb and bloody vomit around her mouth, collapsing in grief.
scenes.turningPointNote: The death of Padraic's beloved donkey represents the ultimate devastating consequence of the conflict

scenes.heading: 124 EXT. VARIOUS - DUSK 124 124
scenes.summary: The sun sets across various scenic spots on the island including the castle ruins and cemetery.

scenes.heading: 125 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DUSK 125 125
scenes.summary: Padraic sits in his chair holding his dead donkey's corpse across his lap while looking at Siobhan's empty chair.

scenes.heading: 126 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DUSK 126 126
scenes.summary: Padraic digs a grave and buries his donkey wrapped in Siobhan's shawl, saying a tearful prayer while the other animals look away.

scenes.heading: 127 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - NIGHT 127 127
scenes.summary: Padraic dresses in funeral clothes, smashes his mirror with an oil lamp, and leaves the house in slow motion.

scenes.heading: 128 EXT. LANEWAYS - NIGHT 128 128
scenes.summary: Padraic encounters Mrs McCormick on the lane and angrily tells her not to put ideas about killing Colm's dog in his head.

scenes.heading: 129 INT. COLM’S HOUSE - NIGHT 129 129
scenes.summary: Padraic enters Colm's empty house, sits with the dog, and reassures it that he would never hurt it because it's the only nice thing about Colm.

scenes.heading: 130 INT. PUB - NIGHT 130 130
scenes.summary: At the pub, Colm happily teaches music to disturbed students despite his bleeding hand, covering their fiddle with blood.

scenes.heading: 131 OMITTED 131 131
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 132 INT. PUB - NIGHT 132 132
scenes.summary: Padraic enters the pub disheveled and bloody, and Colm tells him he doesn't need apologies and they should go their separate ways for good.
scenes.turningPointNote: Colm's final rejection of Padraic solidifies the permanent end of their friendship

scenes.heading: 133 EXT. VARIOUS - DAWN 133 133
scenes.summary: The sun rises over the island and its watery horizon.

scenes.heading: EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAWN 133A
scenes.summary: The sunrise illuminates Jenny's freshly dug grave with its homemade white cross as the sad cows sniff at it.

scenes.heading: 134 EXT. CHURCH - MORNING 134 134
scenes.summary: Church bells ring at nine in the morning calling the islanders to church as they approach from all directions.

scenes.heading: 135 EXT. HIGH LANEWAY TO CROSSROADS - MORNING 135 135
scenes.summary: Colm walks alone with his head bowed past the statue of Mary on his way to church.

scenes.heading: 136 EXT. JETTY - MORNING 136 136
scenes.summary: Peadar meets the priest at the jetty, who notices his black eye as Peadar tells him about it.

scenes.heading: 137 INT. CHURCH - DAY 137 137
scenes.summary: The priest leads a hymn that all islanders sing except Padraic, while Colm notices him sitting a few pews ahead but not looking around.

scenes.heading: 138 EXT. CHURCH - DAY 138 138
scenes.summary: The priest notices that Padraic hasn't greeted him and has quietly ridden away on his pony and cart without speaking.

scenes.heading: 139 INT. CONFESSIONAL - DAY 139 139
scenes.summary: In confession, Colm admits to pride and accidentally killing a miniature donkey, while the priest questions whether God cares about miniature donkeys.

scenes.heading: 140 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 140 140
scenes.summary: Padraic feeds and waters his animals near the donkey's grave, giving them extra food and goodbye kisses as they sense something is wrong, while Siobhan's voiceover letter describes her new life on the mainland.

scenes.heading: 141 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 141 141
scenes.summary: Padraic moves around outside his and Siobhan's bare bedroom picking up items while her voiceover continues, offering him a spare bed and work on the mainland.

scenes.heading: 142 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 142 142
scenes.summary: Padraic loads his pony cart with plywood, driftwood, and oil lamps while Siobhan's voiceover speaks of leaving Inisherin's bleakness behind.

scenes.heading: 143 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 143 143
scenes.summary: Padraic picks up a bucket of paraffin from his empty house with smashed mirror and abandoned books, loads it on his cart, and rides away.

scenes.heading: 144 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DAY 144 144
scenes.summary: Padraic rides away with his cart piled high with inflammables as his animals sadly watch him leave from the road.

scenes.heading: 145 EXT. LAKE - DAY 145 145
scenes.summary: Padraic rides past the lake where Mrs McCormick stands with Dominic's hook pole, staring intently at the water.

scenes.heading: 146 EXT. HILL ABOVE COLM’S HOUSE - DAY 146 146
scenes.summary: Padraic arrives at the hill overlooking Colm's house with its smoking chimney, halts briefly, then continues down toward the house.

scenes.heading: 147 EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - DAY 147 147
scenes.summary: Padraic stacks wood around Colm's house, splashes paraffin on it, saves Colm's dog by putting it on his cart, then smashes lit oil lamps against the house setting it ablaze.
scenes.turningPointNote: Padraic burns down Colm's house in retaliation, escalating their conflict to destructive violence

scenes.heading: INT. COLM’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 147A
scenes.summary: Inside the burning house, Colm calmly smokes a cigarette while framed by the window, and Padraic nods either to himself or to Colm.

scenes.heading: EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 147B
scenes.summary: Padraic rides away on his cart with Colm's dog as the house burns behind them.

scenes.heading: 148 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DUSK 148 148
scenes.summary: At sunset, Padraic's cows look through the window at him inside his lamp-lit house.

scenes.heading: 149 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DUSK 149 149
scenes.summary: Padraic finger-paints with shoe polish on wood while his animals and Colm's sad dog mill around inside his house.

scenes.heading: 150 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DUSK 150 150
scenes.summary: Padraic hangs the painted wood reading 'JENNY' with a heart onto the donkey's crucifix as the sun sets.

scenes.heading: 151 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DUSK 151 151
scenes.summary: Padraic sits alone by candlelight staring into space with the life gone from him, surrounded by his animals and Colm's depressed dog.

scenes.heading: 152 EXT. HILL ABOVE COLM’S HOUSE - DUSK 152 152
scenes.summary: Peadar strides away from Colm's burning house taking out his handcuffs, heading toward Padraic's house.

scenes.heading: 153 EXT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - DUSK 153 153
scenes.summary: Mrs McCormick, drenched and carrying her pole, startles Peadar and points him away from Padraic's house toward the lane.

scenes.heading: 154 EXT. LAKE - DUSK 154 154
scenes.summary: Dominic's bloated drowned body lies in the lake shallows as Mrs McCormick helps drag it with her hook pole while Peadar falls to his knees.
scenes.turningPointNote: Dominic's death by drowning is revealed, eliminating the potential caretaker for Padraic's animals

scenes.heading: 155 EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - DUSK 155 155
scenes.summary: The burning house is shown at sunset from various dangerous angles.

scenes.heading: INT. COLM’S HOUSE - DUSK 155A
scenes.summary: Inside the burning house, Colm's possessions including his clock, fiddle, gramophone, shears, and telescope are engulfed in flames.

scenes.heading: EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - DUSK 155B
scenes.summary: Colm's house totally collapses in on itself.

scenes.heading: 156 INT. PADRAIC’S HOUSE - NIGHT 156 156
scenes.summary: Padraic lies face down in his lonely bed holding Jenny's ribbon and bell, looking at Siobhan's empty bed while his animals watch from the doorway.

scenes.heading: 157 EXT. CASTLE RUINS (DUN AENGUS?) - DAWN 157 157
scenes.summary: Sunrise appears over the castle ruins.

scenes.heading: 158 EXT. GRAVEYARD - DAWN 158 158
scenes.summary: Sunrise appears over the graveyard.

scenes.heading: 159 EXT. HILL ABOVE COLM’S HOUSE - DAWN 159 159
scenes.summary: Padraic walks his animals above the beach near Colm's smouldering house as Colm's dog spots a figure below and runs toward it.

scenes.heading: 160 EXT. BEACH - DAWN 160 160
scenes.summary: Colm gives his dog a happy hug on the beach while Padraic arrives fifteen yards away, with Colm's burned house still smouldering behind them.

scenes.heading: 161 EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 161 161
scenes.summary: Mrs McCormick stands by the burned house observing the two men on the beach, with a smashed window and chair visible outside.

scenes.heading: 162 EXT. BEACH - CONTINUOUS 162 162
scenes.summary: Colm says his burned house makes them quits, but Padraic disagrees since Colm escaped, then Colm reveals his rotting finger pinned back on with bloody thumb tacks.

scenes.heading: 163 EXT. COLM’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 163 163
scenes.summary: Mrs McCormick appears happy at how the confrontation is playing out and lazily hangs her pole across her shoulders.

scenes.heading: 164 EXT. BEACH - CONTINUOUS 164 164
scenes.summary: Padraic starts to leave, Colm thanks him for looking after his dog, Padraic says 'any time,' then continues away as the distance between them grows.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final exchange between Padraic and Colm establishes their permanent estrangement despite a moment of civility

scenes.heading: 165 END 165 165
scenes.summary: The script ends.

structuralWarnings.text: Act 3 is only 14% of the script — structural placement suspect
2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
